title: Example 13-7 spur gear force analysis on idler gear 3

meshes_on_selected_gear.name: force_from_gear_2_on_gear_3
meshes_on_selected_gear.notes: Upper mesh on gear 3. Tangential and radial directions chosen to match the textbook FBD orientation.

meshes_on_selected_gear.name: force_from_gear_4_on_gear_3
meshes_on_selected_gear.notes: Right-side mesh on gear 3. Tangential and radial directions chosen to match the textbook FBD orientation.

notes: Updated to match Shigley Example 13-7 tooth counts: gear 2 = 20 teeth, gear 3 = 50 teeth, gear 4 = 30 teeth.
Speeds are consistent with simple gear ratios: n3 = n2*(N2/N3), n4 = n3*(N3/N4).
All force-direction unit vectors are preserved from the prior file because they are already consistent with the example-style free-body diagram.
